briefdescription.para: Use to provide default behaviour (size negotiation is on, event callbacks are not called). @SINCE_1_2_10.
True if control does not need size negotiation, i.e. it can be skipped in the algorithm @SINCE_1_0.0.
Special marker for last actor flag @SINCE_1_0.0.
Enumeration for the constructor flags. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Value for deriving classes to continue on the flag enum.
Internal owner of this custom actor implementation.
ActorFlags flags to determine behaviour.
Forward declare future extension interface.

detaileddescription.para: Virtual destructor @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Creates a CustomActorImpl. @SINCE_1_0.0.

parameterdescription.para: Bitfield of ActorFlags to define behaviour

briefdescription.para: Requests a relayout, which means performing a size negotiation on this actor, its parent and children (and potentially whole scene).

detaileddescription.para: This method can also be called from a derived class every time it needs a different size. At the end of event processing, the relayout process starts and all controls which requested Relayout will have their sizes (re)negotiated.

@SINCE_1_0.0
RelayoutRequest() can be called multiple times; the size negotiation is still only performed once, i.e. there is no need to keep track of this in the calling side.

briefdescription.para: Provides the Actor implementation of GetHeightForWidth. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The height based on the width

briefdescription.para: Provides the Actor implementation of GetWidthForHeight. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The width based on the height

briefdescription.para: Calculates the size for a child using the base actor object.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
child
The child actor to calculate the size for
dimension
The dimension to calculate the size for. E.g. width or height
Return the calculated size for the given dimension. If more than one dimension is requested, just return the first one found

briefdescription.para: Determines if this actor is dependent on its children for relayout from the base class.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
dimension
The dimension(s) to check for
Return if the actor is dependent on it's children

briefdescription.para: Used by derived CustomActorImpl instances, to access the public Actor interface.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
A pointer to self, or an uninitialized pointer if the CustomActorImpl is not owned.

briefdescription.para: Called after the actor has been connected to the Scene.

detaileddescription.para:
When an actor is connected, it will be directly or indirectly parented to the root Actor. @SINCE_1_9.24
depth
The depth in the hierarchy for the actor
The root Actor is provided automatically by the Scene, and is always considered to be connected. When the parent of a set of actors is connected to the scene, then all of the children will received this callback. For the following actor tree, the callback order will be A, B, D, E, C, and finally F.
A(parent) /\ BC /\\ DEF

briefdescription.para: Called after the actor has been disconnected from the Scene.

detaileddescription.para: If an actor is disconnected, it either has no parent or is parented to a disconnected actor.

briefdescription.para: Called after a child has been added to the owning actor.
Called after the owning actor has attempted to remove a child (regardless of whether it succeeded or not).
Called when the owning actor property is set.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
index
The Property index that was set
propertyValue
The value to set

briefdescription.para: Called when the owning actor's size is set e.g. using Actor::SetSize().
Called when the owning actor's size is animated e.g. using Animation::AnimateTo( Property( actor, Actor::Property::SIZE ), ... ).

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
animation
The object which is animating the owning actor
targetSize
The target size. Note that this target size may not match the size returned via Actor::GetTargetSize

briefdescription.para: Called when animating the owning actor's animatable property. @SINCE_2_4.27.

parameterdescription.para: The object which is animating the owning actor

briefdescription.para: Called when apply constraint property or removed. @SINCE_2_4.41.

parameterdescription.para: Whether it is applied or not.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the off-screen RenderTasks associated with the Actor. This method returns the internal RenderTasks held by the Actor. This tasks are used for off-screen rendering, and the system will assign order index to each tasks based on the render order.

detaileddescription.para: Actor with a non-NONE OffScreenRenderableType should override this method to provide their render tasks.

@SINCE_2_3.43
tasks
A list of RenderTasks to be populated with the Actor's forward or backward off-screen RenderTask.
isForward
Indicates whether to retrieve forward (true) or backward (false) RenderTasks.

briefdescription.para: Sets OffScreenRenderableType of this Actor. This method is called by child class to set type itself.
Register OffScreenRenderableType of this Actor. This method is called by child class to set type itself.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_4.17
offScreenRenderableType
OffScreenRenderableType for this Actor. It could be one of NONE, FORWARD, BACKWARD, and BOTH.
Each FORWARD / BACKWARD type could be added maxium 15 times.
Call UnregisterOffScreenRenderableType() to unregister the type.

briefdescription.para: Unregister OffScreenRenderableType of this Actor. This method is called by child class to set type itself.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_4.17
offScreenRenderableType
OffScreenRenderableType for this Actor. It could be one of NONE, FORWARD, BACKWARD, and BOTH.
Call RegisterOffScreenRenderableType() before unregister the type.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves OffScreenRenderableType of this Actor.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_3.43
OffScreenRenderableType for this Actor.

briefdescription.para: Requests RenderTask reordering when the offscreen properties of this Actor are changed. @SINCE_2_3.43.
Called after the size negotiation has been finished for this control.

detaileddescription.para: The control is expected to assign this given size to itself/its children.
Should be overridden by derived classes if they need to layout actors differently after certain operations like add or remove actors, resize or after changing specific properties.

@SINCE_1_0.0
size
The allocated size
container
The control should add actors to this container that it is not able to allocate a size for
As this function is called from inside the size negotiation algorithm, you cannot call RequestRelayout (the call would just be ignored).

briefdescription.para: Notification for deriving classes.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
policy
The policy being set
dimension
The dimension the policy is being set for

briefdescription.para: Returns the natural size of the actor.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The actor's natural size

briefdescription.para: Calculates the size for a child.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
child
The child actor to calculate the size for
dimension
The dimension to calculate the size for. E.g. width or height
Return the calculated size for the given dimension

briefdescription.para: This method is called during size negotiation when a height is required for a given width.

detaileddescription.para: Derived classes should override this if they wish to customize the height returned.

@SINCE_1_0.0
width
Width to use
The height based on the width

briefdescription.para: This method is called during size negotiation when a width is required for a given height.

detaileddescription.para: Derived classes should override this if they wish to customize the width returned.

@SINCE_1_0.0
height
Height to use
The width based on the width

briefdescription.para: Determines if this actor is dependent on its children for relayout.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
dimension
The dimension(s) to check for
Return if the actor is dependent on it's children

briefdescription.para: Virtual method to notify deriving classes that relayout dependencies have been met and the size for this object is about to be calculated for the given dimension.
Virtual method to notify deriving classes that the size for a dimension has just been negotiated.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
size
The new size for the given dimension
dimension
The dimension that was just negotiated

briefdescription.para: Set this CustomActor is transparent or not without any affection on the child Actors.
Get this CustomActor is transparent or not.
Retrieves the extension for this control.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The extension if available, NULL otherwise

briefdescription.para: Initializes a CustomActor. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The CustomActorImpl is not already owned.
Called when ownership of the CustomActorImpl is passed to a CustomActor.

briefdescription.para: Gets the owner.

detaileddescription.para:
This method is needed when creating additional handle objects to existing objects. Owner is the Dali::Internal::CustomActor that owns the implementation of the custom actor inside core. Creation of a handle to Dali public API Actor requires this pointer. @SINCE_1_0.0
A pointer to the Actor implementation that owns this custom actor implementation

briefdescription.para: Returns whether relayout is enabled. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: Return true if relayout is enabled on the custom actor
Called when ownership of the CustomActorImpl is passed to a CustomActor.

briefdescription.para: CustomActorImpl is an abstract base class for custom control implementations.

detaileddescription.para: This provides a series of pure virtual methods, which are called when actor-specific events occur. And CustomActorImpl is typically owned by a single CustomActor instance; see also CustomActor::CustomActor( CustomActorImpl &implementation ). @SINCE_1_0.0
